throughput_regressions.detail: The current-best 4080 row is still slower than the retained MLX row on raw step throughput, so mixture review must keep accelerator throughput regressions visible instead of hiding them behind source-family prose.
Sample throughput is still below the retained MLX row on the admitted matrix, so weekly mixture review can see the accelerator deficit directly.
Source-token throughput remains below the retained MLX row, which keeps throughput regressions explicit even while exactness and held-out deltas stay flat.

stability_regressions.detail: open adapter backend does not yet support precision policy `Bf16Mixed`
Stale worker remains replay-required with aggregation blocked at timeout 5000 ms.
Upload disagreement still resolves as rejected inside the retained recovery drill instead of silently entering lineage.
Uneven worker speed remains explicit at 18000 ms observed skew.

summary: The executor lane now has one canonical source-family contribution report. It keeps all six source-family weights explicit against baseline-truth slice deltas (exactness, held-out, adversarial all remain flat at zero on the retained baseline), while the run-level section keeps 3 throughput regressions and 4 stability regressions visible on the current-best 4080 row instead of letting mixture review hide infrastructure debt.
